TargetRLF1: A diagnosis of HIV/AIDS can have a profound emotional impact on individuals and can affect their mental well-being and quality of life. Some of the emotional effects commonly associated with HIV/Aids include: Shock and Denial, Fear and Anxiety, Stigma and Discrimination, Grief and Loss, Depression, Anxieties and Worry, Isolation and Loneliness, Guilt and Shame, Loss of Identity, Relationship Challenges and Post-Traumatic Stress. It's important to recognize that these effects are normal reactions to a significant life event like an HIV / AIDS diagnosis. Seeking emotional support and mental health care is essential for managing these emotions and maintaining overall well-Being. Supportive resources or support groups include counseling, counseling for people living with HIV /AIDS, and support networks of friends and loved ones who adhere to emotional support practices. Additionally, emotional support networks can provide emotional support in coping with the emotional challenges associated with living with the virus.

TargetRLF1: Chlamydia also known as Chlamydial Genitourinary Infection often has no symptoms, but it can cause serious health problems, even without symptoms. If symptoms occur, they may not appear until several weeks after having sex with a partner who has chlamydia. In women, common symptoms of genital infection include: a change in vaginal discharge (white, yellow or grey discharge that may be smelly) , bleeding between menstrual periods or after sex, pain or discomfort in the lower abdomen and burning sensation when urinating. Common symptoms of genetic infection in men include: burning while urinating, discharge from the penis (mucus-like or clear watery discharge), pain or malaise in the testicles. Anal infection, in women and men, can cause: pain, mucus- like discharge and bleeding. If a patient has Lymphogranuloma Venereum (LGV), initial symptoms may include genital ulcers, followed by painful swelling of lymph nodes in the groin. If untreated, it can lead to severe complications such as rectal strictures.

TargetRLF1: A chlamydia test detects the bacteria that cause Chlamydia, a sexually transmitted infection (STI) so as to get treatment. Your provider takes a sample of urine or secretions from your vagina, penis or rectum and sends it to a lab for testing. Results are usually ready in a day. Less often, providers use a cell culture to test for chlamydias. In this case, your provider swabs/wipes your vaginal area, urethra or anus to collect a sample and send it to another lab to test. If bacteria are present, it indicates an infection. Providers may choose this type of chlamidia test to detect infections in the anus. Or they may use a cellular culture to see how chlamydiae treatments are working. Because of the possibility of multiple sexually transmission infections, all patients with any sexually transmissible disease (STD) should be evaluated for Chlamydial Screening. There are no risks involved with these tests. Rarely the results of the tests may be inconclusive or unclear, and you may need to give another sample.

TargetRLF1: Medicines are often used to treat first or recurrent episodes of Genital herpes or Herpes Simplex Virus (HSV), also known as Herpes. They can decrease how long symptoms last and how severe they are, but they cant cure the infection. Treatment for recurrent Episodes is most effective when started within 48 hours of when symptoms begin. Antiviral medicines commonly given include acyclovir, famciclovir and valacyclovir. Taking a lower daily dose of one of these medicines can also decreased how often symptoms occur (outbreaks). Treating is often recommended for people who get very painful or frequent recurrent episode or who want to lower the risk of giving herpes to someone else. Medicines to help with pain related to sores include paracetamol (acetaminol acetaminophen), naproxen or popcorn that can be applied to the affected area.• For people who are exposed to cold or fever• Use medicines to avoid painful swollen or active sunscreen• Use oral medicines• Avoid exposure to cold sores• Use condoms• Banana

TargetRLF1: Healthcare providers test your body fluids for the Neisseria gonorrhoeae bacterium. If they detect this bacterium, it means you have gonorrhea. Providers use one of two methods to gather your sample: Swab test. A provider uses a cotton bud to swab the part of your body that might have the infection. They might take a sample from your vagina, cervix, urethra(urine passage), rectum or throat. Urine (pee) test. This involves collecting urine in a specimen container, then taken to the lab for testing.

TargetRLF1: Gonorrhea can be traced back to the earliest records of the human race even if Albert Neisser first described the bacteria gonococcus in 1879. The Romans, Jews and Arabs all have documents referring to gonorrhea and each society had their own description of symptoms and treatment. The origins of Gonorrhoea can be Traced Back to the Old Testament in the books of Leviticus and Genesis where it is described as a contagious, continuous emission of semen and painful erection. The Roman physician Galen in 130 AD described the disease as an "involuntary escape of semen". The word itself derives from the Greek, meaning "the flow of seed" (gono: seed, rhea: flow).

TargetRLF1: Genital Herpes or Herpes Simplex Virus (HSV), also known as herpes is a highly contagious disease. It is transmitted from an infected person to the other through direct contact. Herpes simplex-1 (HS V-1) is predominantly oral, therefore it can be transmitted by kissing an infectated person, getting in contact with saliva of an infecting person and surfaces which are contaminated with the virus. Herpe simplex-2 (HS v-2) is prevalently ano-genital. Transmission of HS V-2 is by contact through unprotected sexual intercourse. All types of sex including oral sex, anal sex, and vaginal sex can result in the spread of HSV-2. Getting in direct contact with vaginal fluids and herpetic sores also can lead to transmission of the herpes virus.

TargetRLF1: Genital Herpes or Herpes Simplex Virus (HSV), also known as Herpes is caused by two types of herpes simplex virus. The genital infection caused by these two viruses manifests in the same way. Type 1 (HS V-1) mostly spreads by oral contact and causes infections in or around the mouth (oral herpes or cold sores). It can also cause genital herpes. Most adults are infected with HS V-1. Type 2 (HS v-2) spreads through sexual contact and causing genitals herpes. However, it is also possible for HS V-2 to cause oral herpes. In rare circumstances, herpes (HSv-1 and HSV-2) can be transmitted from mother to child during delivery, causing neonatal herpes.

If your symptoms are severe and affecting your daily life, talk to your healthcare provider.

TargetRLF1: It is unclear what the origins of the organism Chlamydia trachomatis are. The organism causes both Trachoma, an eye infection and Chlamidia also known as Chlamydial Genitourinary Infection, a Sexually Transmitted Infectance. Chlamidiya trachomitis was first described in historical records in Ebers papyrus written between 1553 and 1550 BC. In the ancient world, it was known as the blinding disease, Trachomas. It seems likely that the organisam evolved with humans and shared a common ancestor with environmental Chlamydiae some 700 million years ago. Theorganism is complex and was initially described as a protozoan in 1907 by Prowazeck and Halberstadter. It was later then thought to be a virus and it is only until 1966 when it was finally correctly understood to be an infection.

TargetRLF1: A chlamydia test detects the bacteria that cause Chlamydia, a sexually transmitted infection (STI) so as to get treatment. Your provider takes a sample of urine or secretions from your vagina, penis or rectum and sends it to a lab for testing. Results are usually ready in a day. Less often, providers use a cell culture to test for chlamydias. In this case, your provider swabs/wipes your vaginal area, urethra or anus to collect a sample and send it to the lab to test. If bacteria are present, it indicates an infection. Providers may choose this type of Chlamydial test to detect infections in the anus. Or they may use a cellular culture to see how chlamydiae treatments are working. Because of the possibility of multiple sexually transmission infections, all patients with any sexually transmitted disease (STD) should be evaluated for Chlamydorax. There are no risks involved with these tests. Rarely, the results of the tests may be inconclusive or unclear and you may need to give another sample.

TargetRLF1: The best way to protect yourself from HIV is to abstain from having sex. However if you cannot abstan, be faithful to your partner, use condoms, if you engage in high risk sexual behaviours then consult your health care provider about Pre-exposure prophylaxis(Prep) medicine taken once a person anticipates being exposed to HIV. Also there is Post exposure prophylactics (PEP) a form ARVs that one takes to prevent acquiring HIV after exposure to a risky situation that lead to HIV infection such as unprotected sexual intercourse, exposure to body fluids of an HIV infected person. Prevention of transmission of HIV from an infected mother to her unborn child is also possible by the mother adhering to her ARV's well during pregnancy, attending antenatal care well and attending care of a qualified birth attendant who implements advanced medical practices to protect the baby from acquiring the HIV virus. Once the baby is born, it is given a preventive syrup and followed up until 18 months of life to ensure it never acquired the virus. Safe delivery of HIV

TargetRLF1: A chlamydia test detects the bacteria that cause Chlamydia, a sexually transmitted infection (STI) so as to get treatment. Your provider takes a sample of urine or secretions from your vagina, penis or rectum and sends it to a lab for testing. Results are usually ready in a day. Because of the possibility of multiple sexually transmission infections, all patients with any sexually transmitted disease (STD) should be evaluated for Chlamydial. There are no risks involved with these tests. Rarely the results of the tests may be inconclusive or unclear, and you may need to give another sample.

TargetRLF1: Human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) is an infection that originated in animals and passed into human hosts. It is closely related to the simian immunodeficient virus (SIV) found in chimpanzees in central Africa. To date, the earliest known case of HIV-1 infection in human blood is from a sample taken in 1959 from a man who died in Kinshasa in what was then the Belgian Congo. SIV probably entered the human population as a result of hunting chimpanzes. It's plausible that transmission could have occurred through a hunter being wounded and blood from a chimpanzee entering his bloodstream. The origin of the Human Immudeficiency Virus (HIV) has been a subject of scientific research and debate since the virus was identified in the 1980s.

TargetRLF1: People with symptoms of oral Genital herpes or Herpes Simplex Virus (HSV), also known as Herpes should avoid oral contact with others (including oral sex) and sharing objects that touched saliva. Individuals with signs of Genital Herpes or herpes Simplex virus (HS V), also know as herpes should abstain from sexual activity while experiencing symptoms. Both HSV-1 and HSV-2 are most contagious when sores are present but can also be transmitted when no symptoms are felt or visible. For sexually active people, consistent and correct use of condoms is the best way to prevent genital herpes and other STIs. Condoms reduce the risk; however, HSV infection can still occur through contact with genital or anal areas not covered by the condom. Medical male circumcision can provide life-long partial protection against HSHPV-2 infection, as well as against HIV and human papillomavirus (HPV). Pregnant women should inform their health care providers about the greatest risk for acquiring neonatal herpes. Preventing acquisition of herpes is particularly important for women in late pregnancy.

Okugatta ku ekyo, omuntu alina obulwadde bwomukono oba obulwadde obwetaagisa okujjanjabibwa mu ngeri eyobuvunaanyizibwa nobujjanjabi bwAkawuka

TargetRLF1: Yee, kya bulijjo okufuna enkyukakyuka mu mbeera z'enneewulira mu kiseera ky'okuvubuka. Kuweka mikono karibu na mdomo wake, kama ilivyoonekana, kunaweza kusaidia kuzuia kuenea kwa virusi vya mdomo

TargetRLF1: Ndiyo, inawezekana kwa mwanamke ambaye hana virusi vya ukimwi kuzaa mtoto aliye na virusi vya Ukimwi. Hali hii inajulikana kama "maambukizi kutoka kwa mama kwenda kwa mtoto" (MTCT) ya virusi vya UKIMWI. Hata hivyo, kwa huduma ya matibabu inayofaa na hatua za kuzuia, hatari ya MTCT inaweza kupunguzwa kwa kiasi kikubwa, na hata inawezekana kuwa na mtoto asiye na virus ya ukimWI. Kwa utunzaji sahihi wa matibabu na kufuata hatua zinazopendekezwa za kuzuia , hatari ya mTCT yanaweza kupunguziwa kwa kiasi kubwa, na wanawake wengi wanaoishi na virusi ya ukIMWI wanaweza kuzaa watoto wasio na virusi bya ukimwie. Lengo la hatua hizi ni kuhakikisha afya na ustawi wa mama na mtoto.

TargetRLF1: Kutumia pre-exposure prophylaxis (PrEP) na post-expouse prophybaxis (PEP) kama inavyoagizwa na mtoa huduma ya afya kwa ujumla ni salama na inafaa kwa kupunguza hatari ya maambukizi ya Ukimwi. Hata hivyo, kutumia kupita kiasi au kutumia vibaya PrEP na PEP kunaweza kuwa na hatari na vikwazo vinavyowezekana. Ili kuongeza manufaa ya PrEP kwa PEP huku ukipunguza hatari, ni muhimu kutumia dawa hizi kama ilivyoagizwa kwa mtoa Huduma ya afya na kulingana na miongozo iliyopendekezwa. Hii ni pamoja na: PrEP inapaswa kutumiwa na watu walio katika hatari kubwa ya kuambukizwa UkimWI, kama vile wale walio na wapenzi wengi wa ngono au mpenzi aliye na UkIMwi. Kupima Ukimwie mara kwa mara na uchunguzi wa kimatibabu pia ni muhimu.

To use PEP effectively, follow these steps: Seek medical care immediately, discuss the situation with a healthcare provider, determine eligibility for PEP, start PEP medications, manage potential side effects, complete the full course, and follow up with testing.

Ni muhimu kukumbuka kwamba watu wengi walio na virus ya ukIMWI huishi maisha marefu na yenye afya kwa matibabu yanayofaa na kujitolea kudumisha ustawi wa jumla.

TargetRLF1: Muda wa maisha wa mtu ambaye ameambukizwa virusi vya ukimwi unaweza kutofautiana kwa kiasi kikubwa kulingana na mambo kadhaa, ikiwa ni pamoja na wakati aligunduliwa, upatikanaji wa huduma za matibabu na matibabu, kuzingatia matibabu , afya kwa ujumla, na uchaguzi wa maisha. virusi vya Ukimwi sio ugonjwa mbaya tena ulivyokuwa hapo awali, shukrani kwa maendeleo ya matibabu pamoja na utunzaji. Kwa utunzajo sahihi wa matibabu. Kufuata matibabu sahihi, na mtindo wa maisha mzuri, watu wengi walio na virusi vya UKIMWI hakika wanaweza kuishi maisha marefu na yenye kuridhisha. Kwa kweli, watu walio na virus ya UkimWI wako kwenye matibabu madhubuti kwa muda wa karibu na ule wa watu kwa ujumla. Hata hivyo, ni muhimu kutambua kwamba hakuna jibu la ukubwa mmoja kwa swali la maisha, kwani linatofautiana kutoka kwa mtu hadi mtu. Ni muhimu kwa watu wanaoishi na virusi wakati wao wa kuishi na watu walio karibu na umri wa kuishi. Uchunguzi wa afya, ufuatiliaji, na watoa huduma wanaopendekezwa kwa ujumla ni hatua muhimu katika kudumisha afya na ustawi wa afya.

TargetRLF1: Wakati mtu anakabiliwa na virusi vya ukimwi kwa bahati mbaya, kuna hatua kadhaa muhimu za kuchukua ili kupunguza hatari ya maambukizi: Post-Exposure Prophylaxis (PEP), Seek Medical Attention, Evaluation and Testing, Start PEP, Follow-Up Care, Behavioral and Psychological Support. Ni muhimu kutambua kwamba wakati PEP inaweza kupunguza kwa kiasi kikubwa hatari ya kuambukizwa virusi vya Ukimwi baada ya mfiduo unaowezekana, haiko dhamana ya ulinzi. Kwa hiyo, ni muhimu kuchukua tahadhari za kuepuka hali zenye hatari kubwa, kama vile kufanya ngono salama na kuchukua hatua za kuzuia majeraha ya shingo au ukosefu wa damu katika mazingira ya huduma ya afya. Kwa muhtasari, ikiwa wewe ni mwathirika wa virusi hivyo, tafuta matibabu mara moja, as early with PEP can be effective in preventing HIV infection. The key is to act promptly, within hours of day or days, as the effectiveness of PEP decreases.

TargetRLF1: Uamuzi wa kushiriki ngono ni wa kibinafsi sana na unaweza kutofautiana kulingana na imani, maadili, kanuni za kitamaduni na hali ya kibinafsi. Hakuna umri wa "haki" au "usio sahihi" wa kuanza kufanya ngono; badala yake, inapaswa kuwa uamuzi unaofanya ukiwa tayari kihisia na kimwili, ukiwa na taarifa za kutosha, na kwa ridhaa ya mwenza wako ikiwa mko kwenye uhusiano wa maelewano. Yafuatayo ni baadhi ya mambo muhimu ya kuzingatia kwa watu binafsi, hasa vijana, wanaofikiria kujihusisha na ngono au kutoshiriki tendo la ndoa: Umri wa Idhini, Kufanya Maamuzi kwa Taarifa, Ridhaa, Ulinzi, Utayari wa Kihisia, Mawasiliano, Shinikizo la Rika, Elimu na Usaidizi, Matokeo. , na Hisaidi kuwa kipekee. Ni muhimu kukumbuka kuwa utayari wa kila mtu kwa shughuli za ngono ni tofauti katika umri wa uzee na ufahamu. Kwa hivyo, ni muhimu kufanya maamuzi maalum kuhusu afya yako ya ngono, ufahamu na ustawi. Haipaswi kamwe kuchukua hatua za kulinda afya yako.

Ni muhimu kukumbuka kuwa utayari wa kila mtu kwa shughuli za ngono ni tofauti katika umri wa uzee na ufahamu. Kwa hivyo, ni muhimu kufanya maamuzi maalum kuhusu afya yako ya ngono, ufahamu na ustawi. Haipaswi kamwe kuchukua hatua za kulinda afya yako.

TargetRLF1: virusi vya ukimwi/UKIMWI si kitu ambacho kilitambulishwa duniani kwa lengo au nia mahususi. virusi vya Ukimwi (Virusi vya Upungufu wa Kinga ya Binadamu) inaaminika kuwa ilitokana na uhamishaji wa virusi kutoka kwa nyani wasio binadamu hadi kwa binadamu, yumkini Afrika ya Kati. Virusi hivyo vinafikiriwa kuwepo kwa wanadamu kwa miongo mingi kabla ya kutambulika na kutambuliwa kama ugonjwa tofauti mapema miaka ya 1980. Ni muhimu kusisitiza kwamba virusi vya ikimwi /UKIMVI ni changamoto kuu ya kimataifa ya afya ya umma, na kuishughulikia kunahitaji mbinu inayohusisha, yenye mambo mengi iliyotambulika, mashirika ya afya, serikali, watafiti, mashirikisho, na mashirika binafsi ya jamii.

TargetRLF1: TB Active can almost always be cured with antibiotics. For pulmonary TB, antibiotics are usually taken daily for six months. For people with TB in other parts of their body, treatment will last longer, perhaps up to 24 months. Latent TB can be treated with preventative treatment to stop it turning into active TB. This is always necessary for people with HIV to prevent TB causing illness and to cure it. It will be a similar course of treatment to active TB . Its important that you take all the pills you are given. If you dont, TB becomes resistant to the drugs, and is much harder to treat.

TargetRLF1: Ni muhimu kuelewa kwamba uke una harufu ya asili, na ni kawaida kabisa kwa kuwa na harufu isiyo ya kawaida na ya musky. Harufu hii inaweza kutofautiana kati ya mtu na mtu na inaweza pia kubadilika katika mzunguko wa hedhi kutokana na mabadiliko ya homoni. Zaidi ya hayo, mambo kama vile chakula, mazoea ya usafi, na shughuli za ngono zinaweza kuathiri harufu za uke. Humuhumu kidogo isiyoambatana na dalili zingine, kama vile kuwasha, kuwaka, kutokwa na uchafu usio wa kawaida au usumbufu, kwa kawaida huchukuliwa kuwa ya mara kwa mara na hakuna kitu cha kuhangaikia. Hata hivyo, ukiona mabadiliko makubwa katika harufu yako ya uke, au ikiwa harufu hiyohiyo inaambatanishwa na dalili kama hizo zilizotajwa hapo juu, inaweza kuonyesha tatizo la msingi, kama ilivyo maambukizi. Maambukizo ya kawaida ambayo yanaweza kusababisha harufu ni pamoja na mabadiliko katika utambuzi wa uke, utoaji wa dawa za kuambukiza, au maambukio ya magonjwa ya vaginosis (STIs). Mara kwa mara, kunaweza kushauriana na matibabu yasiyo ya kawaida, na kuwa

TargetRLF1: Maambukizi ya virusi vya ukimwi wakati wa ngono ya mdomo inachukuliwa kuwa hatari ndogo, lakini sio hatari kabisa. Uwezekano wa maambukiji kimsingi unategemea mambo kadhaa: Aina ya Ngono ya Mdomo, Mzigo wa Virusi, na Matumizi ya Vikwazo. Ingawa hatari ya maambukizo ya virusi ya ukimWI wakati wa mapenzi ya mdomu ni ndogo ikilinganishwa na shughuli nyingine za ngono kama vile kujamiiana bila kinga au kujamiiANA kwa njia ya haja kubwa, si sifuri. Kwa hivyo, ni muhimu kwa watu binafsi kufanya maamuzi sahihi kuhusu shughuli zao za ngono na kuchukua tahadhari ikiwa wana wasiwasi kuhusu maambukuzi ya virus ya ukIMWI. Hatimaye, hatari ya kuambukizwa kwa virusi vya Ukimwi ya ngono za mdomo kwa ujumla sio ndogo ikilinganishwa na tabia zao nyingine, lakini sifuri inapaswa kutafuta mwongozo kuhusu tabia zao zinazofaa za ngono. Watu binafsi wanapaswa kutafuta mwangalifu kutoka kwa mwongozo wa afya ya ngono, watoa huduma za afya, au wahudumu wa afya, ili kufanya maamuzi yanayofaa kuhusu ngono.

TargetRLF1: Mbinu ya kutoa, pia inajulikana kama "kuvuta nje" au "njia ya kuvuta," inahusisha mpenzi wa kiume kutoa uume kutoka kwa uke kabla ya kumwaga ili kuzuia mimba. TargetRLF1: Kwa hakika si lazima kupima Ukimwi. Kuchagua kupimwa ni uamuzi wa kibinafsi unaotegemea wewe, tabia zako na imani yako binafsi. Hakuna sheria inayokupa mamlaka ya kupima UKIMWI. TargetRLF1: Dawa ya Pre-Exposure Prophylaxis (PrEP) ina ufanisi katika kuzuia maambukizi ya virusi vya ukimwi (Virusi vya Upungufu wa Kinga Mwilini) lakini haitoi ulinzi dhidi ya magonjwa ya zinaa (STIs) au kupunguza hatari ya magunduzi ya zinai. PrEP inalenga hasa kuzuia maambulizi ya virusi ya ukIMwi na haina madhara ya moja kwa moja kwa magonjwa mengine ya zinei. Ni muhimu kutambua kwamba wakati wa kutumia PrEP kwa kuzuia virusi vya Ukimwi, bado kuna hatari ya kuambukizwa magonjwa za zinaa kama vile Iliidia, kisonono, kaswende, au herpes. Lakini kujikinga na magonjwa wa zinaa, ni muhimu kuchukua hatua zinazofaa za kuzuia, zikiwemo: Matumizi ya kondomu, kupata vifaa vya kuzuia magonjwa, Mawasiliano ya kawaida na muhtasari kuhusu ngono, na wakati wa kujitolea, PrEP inaweza kusaidia katika kuzuia na kuzuia magundu. Kutumia dawa za kuzuia zinaa ni hatua muhimu katika kuzuia magund

Ingawa juhudi za kisayansi zinazoendelea za kuzuia virusi ni ngumu, juhudi zinazoendelea na lengo la maendeleo ya matibabu ya ukomesha virusi bado zinalenga maendeleo.

Hata hivyo, hakuna njia ya uzazi wa mpango, ikiwa ni kutia ndani kondomu, ambayo ni 100% ya ujinga, na kuna kiwango kidogo cha kushindwa kinachohusishwa na matumizi ya kondomu. Inapotumiwa kwa usaidizi, kondomu ni nzuri sana katika kupunguza hatari za ujauzito na ugonjwa wa zinaa, lakini sio makosa.

TargetRLF1: Shirika la Afya Ulimwenguni (WHO) linaendelea kuchukua jukumu kubwa katika kukabiliana na janga la Ukimwi/UKIMWI duniani kwa kutoa mwongozo, kuweka viwango, na kukuza juhudi za uhamasishaji na kuzuia kote ulimwenguni. Ukimvi/UKİMWI hauchukuliwi kama "ugonjwa wa kienyeji" bali ni jamba la kimataifa ambalo linaathiri jamii na idadi ya watu duniani kote. Kwa hiyo, inahitaji juhudi zilizoratibiwa katika ngazi za kimataifa na za ndani. UKIMWI/UKIIMWI bado ni changamoto kubwa ya afya duniani, na wakati maendeleo yamepatikana katika maeneo mengi, bado kuna kazi kubwa ya kufanya ili kufikia malengo ya UNAIDS 90-90-90, ambayo inalenga kuwa na asilimia 90 ya watu wanaoishi na ukimwi. kujua hali zao, 90% ya watu waliogunduliwa matibabu, na 90% wale wanaopata matibabu wanaelewa kuhusu uandikishaji wa virusi.

TargetRLF1: Super gonorrhea ni neno linalotumiwa kuelezea aina za bakteria Neisseria gonorrhoeae ambazo zimekuwa sugu kwa matibabu ya kawaida, kama vile viuavijasumu kama vile azithromycin na ceftriaxone. Kisonono chenyewe, kiwe kinasababishwa na aina sugu ya dawa au la, kinaweza kuwa na madhara makubwa kiafya kikiachwa bila kutibiwa. Ingawa kisonono kwa kawaida si hatari, inaweza kusababisha matatizo makubwa ya kiafya, hasa iwapo itasambaa katika sehemu nyingine za mwili. Yafuatayo ni baadhi ya matatizo yanayo kusababishwa na ugonjwa wa kisononi ambao haujatibiwa: Ugonjwa wa Kuvimba kwa Pelvic (PID), Utasa, Kuenea kwa Virungo Vingine, Kuongezeka kwa Hatari ya virusi vya ukimwi, Maambukizi ya Gonococcal (GID), pamoja na hatari kubwa. Matatizo muhimu ni kwamba watoto wanaweza kutambua aina hizi za dawa ikiwa ni hatari, hasa ikiwa wanahitaji matibabu yenye ufanisi. Ni muhimu kutambua kwamba matumizi ya aina hizi ya dawa ni muhimu, hasa kwa watu walio na "kinga" na hali ya kinga.
